text: When you write code, you will often need to fix mistakes, or bugs. Identifying bugs is the first step. Start by reading the error messages carefully — they usually tell you what went wrong and where. Using a systematic approach is essential: test small parts of your code one by one instead of checking everything at once. Debugging tools like breakpoints in your editor help you pause the program and inspect values. You can also add logging to print messages that show how your program runs step by step. This helps you understand what happens before a problem appears. With practice, debugging becomes a logical, manageable task.
